description_long: This object is a flintlock-style shotgun with a wooden stock and a short, wide, flared metal barrel, reminiscent of a blunderbuss. The stock is smoothly contoured and finished in a reddish-brown wood grain, while the barrel and lock mechanism are dark metallic, showing some textural detail and rivets. The trigger is exposed and curves downward, with a prominent, ornate trigger guard beneath it. The barrel is relatively short and widens dramatically at the muzzle, suggesting a design for close-range use. The lock mechanism and hammer are visible on the side, with a simple, robust construction. The overall appearance is antique, with a blend of wood and metal materials and a compact, easily handled form.
description: A compact, antique-style shotgun with a flared metal barrel and a smooth wooden stock, featuring ornate metalwork and a prominent trigger guard.
description_view_0: The shotgun is viewed from the rear, showing the rounded end of the wooden stock and the alignment of the barrel.
description_view_1: A side profile reveals the full length of the flared barrel, the wooden stock, the exposed trigger, and the ornate trigger guard.
description_view_2: The front view highlights the wide, flared muzzle of the barrel and the cylindrical shape of the barrel.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile displays the same features as view 1, with the lock mechanism and hammer more visible.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, short and flared with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_action_mechanism: flintlock (lock mechanism and hammer visible, no pump or lever)
attribute_trigger_guard_design: Elongated, rounded trigger guard with ornate curves and a prominent downward extension
